content.summaryText: Major Depression Core Features in Mood Disorders focuses on structured mood episode assessment and risk stratification. Primary objective: Map key symptom clusters.

content.questionText: In Major Depression Core Features, which action best supports 'Map key symptom clusters'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Evaluate mood symptoms, sleep, appetite, and suicide risk
Missing bipolar features before initiating antidepressant plans
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: evaluate mood symptoms, sleep, appetite, and suicide risk. Practical emphasis: Assess episode duration.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when active suicidality, psychosis, severe functional decline, or mania.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
